name: Z' boson from gauged B-L
summary: Gauging the global B-L symmetry of the Standard Model (anomaly-free with three right-handed neutrinos) introduces a new neutral Z' boson coupling proportionally to (B-L) charge. The gauge symmetry is broken at scale v_BL through a SM-singlet scalar phi_BL with B-L charge -2 (giving right-handed Majorana masses M_R = y_N v_BL / sqrt(2) and Z' mass m_Z' = 2 g_BL v_BL). Naturally embeds the seesaw mechanism (M_R from spontaneous B-L breaking) and provides a Z' search channel at LHC. Anomaly-cancellation forces three right-handed neutrinos and links neutrino masses and Z' phenomenology.

addresses_puzzles.note: Right-handed-neutrino Majorana masses arise from spontaneous B-L breaking; gives seesaw scale M_R ~ v_BL naturally. Z' mass and M_R linked via single vev v_BL.

B-L conservation in the gauged U(1)_B-L symmetric phase means sphaleron washout preserves B-L; leptogenesis from N decays naturally produces a B-L asymmetry above v_BL that sphalerons convert to baryon asymmetry.

touches_benchmarks.note: Right-handed Majorana neutrinos with mass M_R from B-L breaking contribute to m_betabeta; KamLAND-Zen and future ton-scale 0nubb experiments probe the seesaw parameter space.

introduces.note: Gauge coupling g_BL, B-L Higgs vev v_BL, and right-handed Yukawa coupling y_N (which sets seesaw mass).

history: Davidson (1979) and Marshak-Mohapatra (1980), PLB 91, 222 - first proposals for U(1)_B-L gauge. Mohapatra-Marshak (1980), 'Local B-L symmetry of electroweak interactions, Majorana neutrinos, and neutron oscillations', PRL 44, 1316 - linked to neutrino-Majorana masses. Modern Z'-B-L phenomenology: Basso, Belyaev, Moretti, Shepherd-Themistocleous (2010), arXiv:1002.3586.

references.citation: Mohapatra and Marshak, 'Local B-L symmetry of electroweak interactions, Majorana neutrinos, and neutron oscillations', Phys. Rev. Lett. 44, 1316 (1980)
Basso, Belyaev, Moretti, Shepherd-Themistocleous, 'Phenomenology of the minimal B-L extension of the Standard Model: Z' and neutrinos', Phys. Rev. D 80, 055030 (2009)
